cards.title: Wall Street Eyes Micron as Next Nvidia
cards.body: Investors are searching for the next AI chip superstar beyond Nvidia. Micron is emerging as a top candidate in the competitive semiconductor market.

cards.title: Skepticism Surrounds Elon's Orbital Data Centers
cards.body: SoftBank's CEO joins critics questioning Elon Musk's orbital data center vision. Not everyone believes the hype around space-based AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Apple Vision Pro Executive Joins OpenAI
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's Vision Pro VP, is departing for OpenAI's hardware team. The move signals OpenAI's continued expansion into physical devices.

cards.title: Founder Uses Claude AI to Fight Cancer
cards.body: Entrepreneur Connor Christou leveraged Claude to analyze his cancer battle data. He fed blood results, scans, and wearables into the AI system.

cards.title: Asian Startups Launch Mythos Alternatives
cards.body: New Asia-based AI models promise Mythos-like capabilities without export restrictions. U.S. AI labs risk losing massive international market share.

cards.title: Trump Admin Releases Anthropic Mythos Widely
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and agencies gain authorized access to Mythos 5. Authorization includes non-American employees across approved organizations.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 After Gov Request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6 rollout following government pressure. The company argues such restrictions shouldn't become standard practice.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief
cards.body: OpenAI recruits Uber India's leader to expand operations in the subcontinent. The hiring signals aggressive market growth outside the U.S.

cards.title: Tech Giants Build Custom AI Chips
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are developing proprietary chips to reduce Nvidia dependence. OpenAI's Jalapeño chip, built with Broadcom, marks the latest challenge.

cards.title: AI Competition Has Real Political Stakes
cards.body: Advanced AI models now carry significant political consequences. Industry leaders must address these implications through collective action.

cards.title: OpenAI's Jalapeño Chip Challenges Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom. The development marks a pivotal shift away from Nvidia's market dominance.

cards.title: White House Requests GPT-5.6 Slow Rollout
cards.body: The Trump administration asked OpenAI to delay public GPT-5.6 release over safety concerns. OpenAI plans selective partner access instead of broad availability.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for Agent Testing
cards.body: The ex-Meta startup secures funding to build digital worlds for AI stress-testing. Demand for agent evaluation tools is reportedly soaring.

cards.title: Claude Gains Ground on Paid ChatGPT Users
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning paying consumers from ChatGPT. Data shows growing preference for Claude among premium subscribers.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Gaming AI
cards.body: The startup secured funding to train AI agents on millions of hours of gameplay. Gaming-based learning could develop more human-like AI intuition.

cards.title: Databricks Cuts AI Power Consumption Dramatically
cards.body: Un-0 image-generation tool demonstrates a 1,000x potential power bill reduction. Databricks' former AI chief is leading the efficiency breakthrough.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M Series A from a16z
cards.body: Netris provides software for network switches powering AI neoclouds. The funding accelerates deployment timelines for neocloud infrastructure.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs
cards.body: Adobe completed acquisition of image and video enhancement tool maker Topaz Labs. Integration across Adobe apps is planned.

cards.title: Apple VP joins OpenAI's hardware team
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple Vision Pro's executive, is leaving to join OpenAI's hardware division. The move signals OpenAI's expanding ambitions beyond software into physical devices.

cards.title: AI helps founder fight cancer with data
cards.body: Connor Christou fed his medical data—blood results, scans, wearables, and journals—into Claude to combat his cancer diagnosis. The AI-assisted approach demonstrates potential medical applications of large language models.

cards.title: Asian startups launch models amid export ban
cards.body: New AI models with Mythos-like capabilities are launching across Asia as Anthropic faces export restrictions. This could result in significant market losses for U.S. AI labs.

cards.title: Government expands Anthropic Mythos access widely
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies are now authorized to use Mythos 5, including non-American employees. The expansion reflects government confidence in the model's capabilities.

cards.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after pressure
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6 access following a government request, stating such restrictions shouldn't become standard. The company argues that limiting distribution prevents tools from reaching users who need them most.

cards.title: OpenAI hires Uber India chief for growth
cards.body: OpenAI recruited the Uber India chief to lead operations in its largest market outside the U.S. The hire marks aggressive expansion of offices, partnerships, and hiring in India.

cards.title: Tech giants build custom chips challenging Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are building proprietary AI chips as alternatives to Nvidia's dominance. OpenAI's Jalapeño chip, built with Broadcom, signals a shift away from total chip market dependence.

cards.title: AI capability now has real political stakes
cards.body: As AI models grow more powerful, their capabilities create significant political consequences. Addressing these impacts requires collective action beyond individual company competition.

cards.title: White House asks OpenAI to slow rollout
cards.body: The Trump administration requested OpenAI slow GPT-5.6 release to a select group due to safety concerns. OpenAI will distribute the model to partners rather than the broader public.

cards.title: Patronus AI raises $50M for agent testing
cards.body: The agent-testing startup, founded by former Meta AI researchers, secured $50M to build digital worlds for stress-testing AI agents. Investor demand for the platform's capabilities is described as "nearly insatiable."

cards.title: Claude gains ground against ChatGPT
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paid consumers despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead. Data shows a notable shift in preferences among paid AI users.

cards.title: Gaming startup raises $320M for AI training
cards.body: General Intuition bet $320M that video game footage can train AI agents for real-world tasks. The company believes action data from gameplay helps AI develop something closer to human intuition.

cards.title: Former Databricks AI chief cuts power usage
cards.body: Un-0 is an image-generation tool that reportedly reduces AI power consumption by 1,000x compared to conventional systems. The technology demonstrates how efficiency breakthroughs could reshape AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Netris raises $15M for AI infrastructure speed
cards.body: The a16z-backed startup provides network switch software to help neocloud operators accelerate time-to-live. Netris is addressing critical infrastructure bottlenecks in AI deployment.

cards.title: Adobe acquires Topaz Labs for AI tools
cards.body: Adobe acquired image and video enhancement maker Topaz Labs to integrate tools across its applications. The acquisition strengthens Adobe's AI capabilities in creative software.

cards.title: AI reshapes retail behind the scenes
cards.body: AI is transforming retail operations—not just through flashy chatbots, but by optimizing product search, inventory decisions, and customer recommendations. The biggest changes happen in systems consumers never see.

cards.title: Stripe, Anthropic, OpenAI fight infections
cards.body: The companies are backing an effort to prevent respiratory infections like the common cold. Strategic partnerships between AI leaders and health innovators could accelerate medical breakthroughs.

cards.title: Web data infrastructure layer emerges for AI
cards.body: Enterprises need data at scale for AI, but much information remains blocked or unstructured. A new infrastructure layer is emerging to unlock data for AI models.

cards.title: NYT slams Microsoft for infringing copyright
cards.body: The New York Times accused Microsoft of building a copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI. The legal dispute highlights tensions over AI training data and intellectual property.

cards.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of Claude cloning
cards.body: Anthropic claims Alibaba executed the largest Claude cloning attack and demands punishment. The incident underscores security and IP protection challenges in AI competition.

cards.title: Notion shuts email app as AI dominates
cards.body: Notion is killing its email app because most users prefer AI agents for communication tasks. The shift reflects changing user preferences as AI assistants become more capable.
